0.body: HyperBeetle is a fan-made tribute to Thumper . It supports custom levels and modding.
0.title: HyperBeetle

1.body: HyperBeetle source and documentation are released under the GNU General Public License v3.0 .
1.title: License

10.body: Since the shader stages are combined. HB_VERT is defined if the source is being used as vertex shader code. and HB_FRAG is defined if the source is being used as fragment shader code. HB_GENERATED will be defined if the code was generated by the engine preprocessor.
10.breadcrumbs: Custom GLSL stntax » Extra preprocessor defined
10.title: Extra preprocessor defined

11.body: The version may not be specified. The engine will do this for you.
11.breadcrumbs: Custom GLSL stntax » Removed version directive
11.title: Removed version directive

12.body: You may include other code by using #include <name>. This does NOT refer to a file name. This refers to the name assigned to the a source code block in the engine. ARB_shading_language_include is not used to implment this feature.
12.title: Include support

13.body: GLFW 3.3.8 Glad 2 OpenGL 3.3 Core w/ KHR_debug Dear ImGui 01b99a9 glm 4afe953b Tracy Profiler v0.10 Boost Regex 1.82.0 Lua 5.4.6 (My own unofficial mirror) ImGuizmo stb_image v2.29 stb_vorbis.c f4a71b1 MINIAUDIO v0.11.21 Assimp v5.0.1 NanoVG f93799c Spdlog v1.13.0
13.title: Dependencies

14.body: GLM receently released 1.0.0 ; Should consider updating. Our assimp is a little dated, should consider updating it soon
14.title: Update notes

16.body: HyperBeetle uses the Tracy Profiler . A Profiler is used to gather detailed frame information. This helps us to find hot spots in our code. To use the profiler simply attach Tracy to HyperBeetle.
16.title: Profiling

17.body: We have built in support for renderdoc. Meaning if the application is luanched via renderdoc, we can interact with renderdoc. Additionally if the application is launched outside of renderdoc. The engine will attempt to automcatically attach it.
17.title: RenderDoc

18.body: This applies when the application was not ran via renderdoc. Windows currently supports this better.
18.title: Renderdoc search other

2.body: This project has all dependencies either directly in the source tree or as git submodules. Make sure you clone with submodules. --recurse-submodules. Uses Premake . Have this installed. premake5 vs2022 to generate a Visual Studio solution for Windows. premake5 gmake2 to generate makefiles.
2.title: Building

21.body: The application disables the renderdoc gui. This ui gets quite intrusive when using the builtin tooling. Renderdoc has a debug ui which may be accessed in the engine.
21.title: Ui changes

22.body: F12 and PRTSC are commonly used to take screenshots, thus these defaults of RenderDoc are changed. Use F2 to take a frame capture.
22.title: Frame capture

23.body: The debug ui will contains a list of captures. If you're running windows. You can simply click an item in this list to open renderdoc.
23.title: Opening captures

24.body: Thumper uses the Futura_PT font. Due to liscencing this font cannot be redistributed. The first font loaded should be the english font. The first loaded font is conditionally chosen.
24.title: Fonts

25.body: fonts/Futura_PT_Heavy.ttf fonts/NotoSans-Bold.ttf Additionally more fonts may be loaded to support more languages. Currently fonts/NotoSansKR-Bold.ttf is loaded. To do something else edit the Fonts global inside scripts/config.lua.
25.title: First font load

26.body: leaf_format thumper editor values and things thumper editor objects
26.title: Additonal Documents

27.body: Add WinMain support. Better language support.

3.body: HyperBeetle uses a modified version of glsl. This is to allow shaders to be reasoned about much easier.
3.breadcrumbs: Custom GLSL stntax » Custom GLSL syntax
3.title: Custom GLSL syntax

30.breadcrumbs: Credits and Contributors » Sound effects / Sound samples
30.title: Sound effects / Sound samples

4.body: Reduce the requirement to have separate shaders for each shader stage. Allow us to reuse code and use features such as #include.
4.title: Motivation

6.body:
Functions may use C++ style annotations. [[vert]] and [[frag]]. Annotation a function with one of these will only include it in that shader type. [[vert]] void main(void) {}
[[frag]] void main(void) {}
6.title: Function annotations

7.body: Tokens match premake syntax for tokens. They are simply placeholders that get swapped at compiletime. %{engine_version} MAY get changed to 1.0.0.
7.title: Tokens

8.breadcrumbs: Custom GLSL stntax » in / out / varying modifications
8.title: in / out / varying modifications

9.body: Layout qualifiers are not supported. Instead we assign an int to the input and output types. This will generate the associated layout qualifers, or use a fallback.
9.breadcrumbs: Custom GLSL stntax » in / out assignment
9.title: in / out assignment
